title: SpaceX Just Had the Biggest IPO in History — $135 a Share Today
script: SpaceX just went public today! This morning, SpaceX started trading on Nasdaq under the ticker SPCX. The IPO price: $135 per share — a flat, take-it-or-leave-it number. The total valuation: $1.77 trillion. That makes this the biggest IPO in history, bigger than Saudi Aramco in 2019. SpaceX raised $75 billion by selling 555 million new shares. Unlike most IPOs, there was no bidding range — Elon Musk's company set a fixed price and investors either bought in or they didn't. SpaceX also absorbed xAI — Musk's artificial intelligence company — and rebranded it SpaceXAI last month. Analysts set price targets ranging from $63 to $190 per share. At $135, the market is betting this space venture becomes the most valuable company on Earth. At $135 a share, would you buy a piece of SpaceX today?
